mechanism_detail: Pure deterministic rule engine. Zero LLM/inference calls anywhere in src/. Grep for 'openai(', 'Anthropic(', 'api.openai', 'api.anthropic' returns zero matches. The strings 'openai', 'anthropic', 'claude' appear ONLY as keywords/topics/ownerPrefixes inside static rule tables (rules.ts, tag-rules.ts). No embeddings, no classification model, no second pass.
mechanism_notes: AI-focus tag layer (multi-label, flat, orthogonal to category): src/lib/pipeline/classification/classifier.ts -> deriveTags(repo)
Rule tables: src/lib/pipeline/classification/rules.ts (15 category rules) + src/lib/pipeline/classification/tag-rules.ts (8 tag rules)
Pipeline integration: src/lib/pipeline/pipeline.ts:198-210 calls classifyBatch + deriveTags and projects primary.categoryId + tags onto each Repo. Only called from the live pipeline pass.

README claim of '10 categories (AI/LLM, Dev Tools, Infra, Web3, Frontend, Backend, Data, Security, DevOps, Other)' DOES NOT match code. Actual category universe is a 15-member union (PipelineCategoryId in src/lib/pipeline/types.ts:182-198) with a 10-member UI surface in src/lib/constants.ts:9-90. README is wrong/stale.
category_list_mismatch_note: The classifier can emit categoryId 'ai-agents', 'mcp', 'browser-automation', 'local-llm', or 'design-engineering' — none of which exist in the UI CATEGORIES list. The UI category page/filter will not render them unless something downstream remaps them. This is a silent bug: a repo correctly classified as 'ai-agents' (e.g., langchain-ai/langchain, microsoft/autogen) will be invisible under category filtering that iterates CATEGORIES.
precedence: A single unified scoring pass across ALL rules (no cascading override stages). The classifier does NOT consult the seed list. Seed-list category (src/lib/seed-repos.ts) only controls ingestion ordering/chunking — it never overrides the classifier. Proof: src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/seed.ts flattens SEED_REPOS into an unannotated owner/name list; src/lib/pipeline/adapters/normalizer.ts:41 stamps every repo with categoryId='other' on arrival; src/lib/pipeline/pipeline.ts:198-210 then overwrites with classifyRepo(repo).primary.categoryId derived purely from GitHub-fetched topics/description/name/owner.

rules.logic: If repo.owner === prefix (case-insensitive) OR repo.fullName startsWith `${prefix}/` OR repo.fullName === prefix OR repo.fullName startsWith prefix — add +50 raw points (largest single signal). First prefix in rule.ownerPrefixes that hits wins (break).
rules.matches: Strongest signal per rule (SCORE_OWNER_PREFIX = 50). Single rule can only score owner once.

rules.logic: For each topic in rule.topics: if topicsLower.includes(topic.toLowerCase()) -> +30 raw points PER matched topic. Cumulative across all topic matches for that rule.
rules.matches: Second-strongest signal per match but can dominate cumulatively (e.g., 3 topic hits = 90).

rules.logic: For each keyword in rule.keywords: if description.toLowerCase().includes(keyword.toLowerCase()) -> +15 raw points. Then SEPARATELY for name: if name.toLowerCase().includes(keyword.toLowerCase()) -> +10. Note: description and name are ADDED (not max), so a keyword in both name + description = +25 for one keyword.
rules.file_ref: src/lib/pipeline/classification/classifier.ts:80-95, constants SCORE_KEYWORD_DESC=15 and SCORE_KEYWORD_NAME=10 at lines 37-38
rules.matches: Substring match, not word-boundary. 'react' inside 'reactor' or 'reactivity' counts.

rules.logic: See precedence 3 — same loop, separate SCORE_KEYWORD_NAME=10. Shared with description inside same loop.
NOT CONSULTED. The classifier does not read repo README content. Only topics, description, name, owner, fullName are inspected.
rules.matches: A repo with a 20-page README calling itself 'the best AI agent framework' but with zero AI-adjacent topics, description, or owner signal will fall through to the devtools fallback.

rules.logic: DOES NOT EXIST. The SeedCategoryId bucket a repo lives in (ai-ml, crypto-web3, etc.) has zero influence on classification. Seed buckets only drive ingestion order and cron chunking in src/lib/pipeline/ingestion/seed.ts. A repo placed in SEED_REPOS.ai-ml with misleading topics will still be mis-classified by the classifier.
rules.matches: This is the #1 source of user surprise: putting a repo in 'ai-ml' in seed-repos.ts does NOT guarantee it lands in ai-ml category on the site.

rules.logic: DOES NOT EXIST. Zero calls to openai/anthropic/any LLM SDK. Grep-verified.

scoring_math.weight_note: ai-ml has the LOWEST weight (1.0) despite being the most-populated category in the seed list. This means a repo with ai-ml topics + rust-ecosystem topics will be classified rust-ecosystem (weight 1.1) even if ai-ml is the stronger semantic fit. See .data/categories.jsonl line 282 meilisearch/meilisearch: primary=databases not ai-ml.

thresholds.primary_min_raw_score_file_ref: src/lib/pipeline/classification/classifier.ts:41 PRIMARY_MIN_CONFIDENCE=0.1 (compared against rawScore, not confidence — misleading constant name)

tie_breakers: No explicit tie-break. scored.sort((a,b) => b.rawScore - a.rawScore) is a stable sort, so the first rule in CLASSIFICATION_RULES order wins on ties. Declared order in rules.ts: ai-agents, mcp, devtools, browser-automation, local-llm, security, infrastructure, design-engineering, ai-ml, web-frameworks, databases, mobile, data-analytics, crypto-web3, rust-ecosystem. So ties favor ai-agents > mcp > devtools > ... > rust-ecosystem. Tie-break is implicit/positional, not principled.
multi_category_handling: Each repo gets ONE primary categoryId (the top rawScore rule) and up to 2 secondary matches filtered by confidence >= 0.15. Only primary is projected onto Repo.categoryId; secondary is persisted to .data/categories.jsonl but not surfaced in UI filters. Orthogonal multi-label tagging happens via deriveTags(repo) which returns all matching tag ids from TAG_RULES — this is what the AI-focus UI chips consume (TagsBar.tsx).

failure_modes.no_topics_no_keywords_no_owner_match: Returns fallback { categoryId: 'devtools', confidence: 0, matched: {topics:[], keywords:[], ownerPrefix:null} }. Example in persisted data: meta-llama/llama (line 14), karpathy/nanogpt (line 16), karpathy/llm.c (line 17), triton-lang/triton (line 23), deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3 (line 24), dottxt-ai/outlines (line 27), ggml-org/whisper.cpp (line 28). All 7 are obviously AI/ML but GitHub returns them with thin or missing topics so they collapse into 'devtools' fallback. This is the single biggest silent miss.
failure_modes.conflicting_topics: Highest weighted (rawScore*weight) rule wins. ollama/ollama ends up local-llm (weight 1.6) not ai-ml (weight 1.0) because owner prefix + topic ollama fires local-llm rule first. Correct outcome here, but the weight hierarchy was clearly tuned before the AI-focus recalibration — ai-ml at 1.0 is too low for 2026.
failure_modes.ai_agents_vs_ai_ml_collision: langchain-ai/langchain correctly classified ai-agents (line 2). But langchain-ai/langgraph and transitive-bullshit/agentic are not in persisted data at all — classifier hasn't run on them since the seed expansion.
failure_modes.readme_only_repos: Any repo whose AI nature is evident only from README body (not topics or short description) is missed entirely.
failure_modes.new_topics: Rule tables are frozen; a newly-minted GitHub topic (e.g., 'agentic-coding', 'sub-agent') fires nothing until a human edits rules.ts.
failure_modes.empty_description_empty_topics: Very common for freshly-minted hot repos; they all fall to devtools:0.

misses.note: Persisted .data/categories.jsonl contains 309 lines all timestamped 2026-04-17T11:07:48Z — a single classification run that predates the 'Fix AI-focus: broaden tag rules + add Claude Code / MCP / agent seed repos' commit. Until the pipeline re-runs, NONE of the Claude Code / MCP / agent ecosystem is persisted.

misses.description: Any repo whose AI/agent identity lives primarily in README (not topics or description) will fall to devtools:0. Observed in persisted data: karpathy/nanoGPT, karpathy/llm.c, triton-lang/triton, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3, meta-llama/llama, meta-llama/llama3, ggml-org/whisper.cpp, dottxt-ai/outlines — all canonical ML repos misclassified as devtools.

false_positives.description: Keyword 'defi' substring matches 'defining', 'default', etc. Line 3 huggingface/transformers has secondary crypto-web3:0.195 from 'defi' substring. Line 96 docker/compose has the same crypto-web3:0.195 secondary.
Keyword 'react' matches 'reactive', 'reactor'. Every bundler (esbuild, bun, swc) picks up web-frameworks:0.39 secondary via 'react' topic being set on their repos.

All anthropics/* repos auto-classify as MCP even when they're SDKs, courses, or cookbooks. Should be split: claude-code itself -> ai-agents or new 'claude-code' category; SDKs -> ai-ml; courses -> a new 'education' bucket or just ai-ml.

huggingface/smolagents correctly an agent framework but owner prefix forces it to ai-ml (weight 1.0). Only fixable by (a) adding huggingface/smolagents to ai-agents ownerPrefixes, or (b) making keyword/topic signal override owner.
open-webui shows secondary mcp:0.6 from topic 'mcp' — correct. But jaegertracing/jaeger falls to devtools:0. These thin signals pollute the secondary list without adding user value.

confidence_scoring: rawScore is sum of topic + keyword + owner points times rule.weight. confidence = clamp(rawScore/100, 0, 1). Mental model: one owner hit on a weight-1 rule = confidence 0.5. One topic + one keyword-in-desc = confidence 0.45 on a weight-1 rule. Three topics = 0.9. The 100-point normalizer means ai-ml rule (weight 1.0) needs 3+ topic hits to reach 'high' (>=0.6) confidence, while mcp rule (weight 2.0) only needs 1 topic (+30 * 2.0 = 60 -> 0.6 high). Confidence is not calibrated probability — it's a capped score.
tie_breakers_detail: scored.sort stable by rawScore desc. First rule in CLASSIFICATION_RULES wins on equal rawScore. Declared order places ai-agents first, which is a deliberate bias toward AI classification — but it only matters on exact ties, which are rare. Tags (deriveTags) are order-independent via Set.

estimated_recall_on_ai.OSSInsight_AI_Agent_Frameworks_reasoning: OSSInsight's canonical AI Agent Frameworks collection contains roughly: langchain, langgraph, autogen, crewAI, AutoGPT, babyagi, SuperAGI, OpenHands, smolagents, pydantic-ai, dspy, LlamaIndex (run-llama/llama_index), OpenInterpreter, instructor, SWE-agent, agentic (transitive-bullshit), gpt-researcher, AgentGPT, mem0, letta, storm, swarms, smol-developer, Eliza (elizaOS), browser-use, aider, continue, cline, goose (block), anything-llm, LibreChat, cody, open-interpreter, graphrag, pydantic-ai, Roo-Code, cursor, markitdown, ChatDev, camel, metagpt, agentverse, ~30-50 total. Of these, roughly 60-70% are in starscreener seed. But only ~15 are PERSISTED with a category right now (the others are post-commit additions awaiting a classifier run). Of those 15 persisted and classified correctly as AI-family (ai-agents/ai-ml/local-llm/mcp), recall against OSSInsight is ~30-35%. After pipeline re-runs, assuming rule tables aren't changed, recall rises to maybe ~55-65% (still missing aider, eliza, browser-use, markitdown, metagpt, ChatDev, camel entirely — they're not seeded — and miss-classifying SDKs/courses under mcp, cline/roo under devtools).
estimated_recall_on_ai.OSSInsight_AI_Agent_Frameworks_ceiling: Upper bound ~75% recall without adding new seed repos and without broadening owner prefixes. Main obstacles: (1) ~10 top-tier repos not seeded at all, (2) classifier ignores README, so repos with thin topics fall to devtools, (3) ai-ml weight of 1.0 drags the category behind local-llm and ai-agents.

gaps: LLM inference is absent. Zero openai/anthropic SDK calls. The 'Fix AI-focus' commit did NOT add LLM assistance — it expanded rule tables + seed list only.
README content is not parsed. This is the single biggest recall gap: many AI repos have minimal GitHub topics because maintainers don't set them.
.data/categories.jsonl is stale — predates the latest seed expansion. The AI-focus story the product tells is mostly invisible until the pipeline runs again on the full, expanded seed.

Classifier category universe (15) diverges from UI category universe (10). ai-agents, mcp, browser-automation, local-llm, design-engineering can be emitted but may not be selectable/filterable in the UI. Verify UI route handling for these — if /category/ai-agents returns 404 or empty, that's a silent 5-category dead zone.

Seed-list bucket is NOT used as a prior for classification. A repo curated into SEED_REPOS.ai-ml still gets categoryId=devtools if GitHub returns empty topics. Trivial fix: pass SeedCategoryId as a fallback when classifier confidence is 0.

Tag rules and classification rules have divergent owner prefix lists. mem0ai is in tag rules (agent-memory) but NOT in classification rules (ai-ml) — so the repo gets a tag but its primary category can still be wrong. These tables should share a common source of truth or at least be consistency-checked.
Rule weights frozen and skewed. ai-ml=1.0 is too low for 2026 — in practice, rust-ecosystem (1.1) beats ai-ml when both fire. Weight ai-ml at 1.3 minimum.

Owner prefix 'anthropics' forces EVERY anthropics/* repo into mcp category. Need either (a) narrower prefix like 'anthropics/claude-code' only, or (b) subtract mcp rule weight when no mcp topic is present.
Owner prefix 'microsoft/autogen' is a hardcoded exact path — inconsistent with other ownerPrefixes which are just 'owner'. Works due to the `fullName.startsWith(prefix)` fallback but fragile.
No test case for the devtools fallback actually failing. tests/classification.test.ts only tests happy paths.
Substring matching is naive: 'defi' hits 'defining', 'react' hits 'reactor'. No word boundary, no tokenization.

Browser-automation and design-engineering CATEGORIES exist but are under-seeded. browser-use/browser-use is not in SEED_REPOS at all. shadcn/ui is not in the seed either despite design-engineering having an owner prefix for 'shadcn-ui'.
